mainFeedback.title: Samira: stop giving wins back
mainFeedback.focus: Honest read: your biggest leak is not damage. It is refusing to cash out: staying at lethal HP, re-fighting after winning, and chasing sideways when structure is already open.
mainFeedback.rule: If the next action does not create HP safety, wave crash, turret, or nexus pressure, do not take it.
mainFeedback.nextRep: Next game: cash out before the second fight.
mainFeedback.whyTrust: The clips show the same leak in three forms: lethal-HP lane stay, low-health re-fight, and open-base drift. That is specific enough to practice immediately.
mainFeedback.pattern: You already find fights. The coachable problem is making the second decision boring enough to actually win: reset, crash, hit tower, hit nexus, then fight again only if it protects that payout.
checklist: First win becomes wave or structure.
Open base means no sideways chase.
mainFeedback.reviewLimit: Replay review is based on sampled frames and visible state, not raw inputs or full cooldown telemetry.

recordings.title: Overstay After Winning Fight at Inhibitor
recordings.feedbackTitle: Overstay After Winning Fight at Inhibitor
recordings.feedback: Mistake: Stayed mid to farm and re-engage after breaking open the inhibitor, instead of resetting with the team for tempo and item power. Fix: Immediately recall after taking down the inhibitor with your team, then spend gold and look for the next play from base rather than farming more mid waves or forcing a risky re-entry.
recordings.gameDetail: This leads to you lingering mid and farming solo rather than recalling in sync with your team and protecting your shutdown and tempo. The consequence: you don’t leverage your gold lead into immediate item advantages, leave your window for coordinated pressure open for a potential respawned defense, and risk getting caught alone, which at higher level would mean a throw and loss of map pressure. Lesson: Anytime you break the base and your team gives disengage signals or resets, do not waste time solo-farming mid—reset immediately and look for the next coordinated play.
recordings.eventEvidence: 11:18 Shows Samira and team inside enemy base post-inhibitor take, with other teammates disengaging while Samira remains mid — key evidence for overstaying after breaking open inhib instead of resetting with team for tempo.
recordings.goodThing: Strong conversion and presence in base-breaking fight with team, enabling objective.
recordings.whyTrust: Resetting the moment you break the inhibitor and the team leaves denies comeback shutdowns and syncs power spikes for the next push; the frames show lingering after the win, losing momentum and risking solo picks.
recordings.evidence: 11:18 Shows Samira and team inside enemy base post-inhibitor take, with other teammates disengaging while Samira remains mid — key evidence for overstaying after breaking open inhib instead of resetting with team for tempo.
recordings.pattern: After winning a heavy fight and breaking the base, Alan continues to linger and farm instead of instantly resetting for items and coordinated next steps, exposing himself to solo deaths and missing a chance to press advantage with gold spent.
recordings.diamondRule: When your team disengages after breaking an inhibitor, instantly recall—never solo-farm or push another mid wave unless you see clear numbers and vision advantage.
recordings.drill: Next queue: As soon as inhib falls and most of your team starts leaving base, press B (recall) instantly and spend your gold before looking at side waves or more farm.

recordings.title: Base win becomes side chase
recordings.feedbackTitle: Base win becomes side chase
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after the base was cracked, you kept accepting second and third fights away from the cleanest payout. Fix: after inhibitor/base pressure, call the state out loud: free structure, body blocking structure, or reset; only fight if it directly protects one of those.
recordings.gameDetail: At 20:58 you are already in the enemy base with an open lane and enough damage to keep the push moving; that part is good objective pressure, not the mistake. At 21:14 the fight has paid you, two allies are dead, and you are holding about 2700 unspent gold, so the next Master-climb decision is to reset or regroup instead of staying available for the next collapse. At 22:27 you run down bot side alone with the base still cracked, which leaks the map win into travel time and gives enemies another respawn window. The clearest mistake is the 27:08 to 27:12 chase into the side jungle after the base is already open; by 27:56 you are back in the base brawl at low HP with multiple defenders alive, which is exactly how a won beginner-bot base sequence becomes a shutdown or stall in harder games. The lesson is not stop fighting forever; it is stop fighting unless the fight removes the body between you and the structure.
recordings.eventEvidence: 20:58 Samira is in the enemy base hitting through minions and defenders. 21:14 shows the won fight plus about 2700 unspent gold. 22:27 shows a solo bot-side drift after base pressure. 27:08 to 27:12 shows the chase away from structures. 27:56 shows the low-HP re-entry into multiple defenders.
recordings.goodThing: You repeatedly found the base and created real ending pressure; the mistake is what happened after the first payout, not the fact that you went to objectives.
recordings.whyTrust: The evidence separates the good habit from the leak: you did get to the enemy base, but the later side chase and low-HP re-entry are the parts that stop the same lead from converting cleanly against stronger players.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame review of the queued May 21 full-game clip: visible base pressure at 20:58 and 21:14, solo bot-side drift at 22:27, side-jungle chase at 27:08-27:12, and low-HP base re-entry at 27:56.
recordings.pattern: The recurring leak is post-win state switching. You win enough of the first fight, then spend the next seconds on a side chase, extra body, or low-HP re-entry instead of choosing reset, regroup, or structure.
recordings.diamondRule: After a base win, every click must be one of three things: hit free structure, kill the body blocking structure, or reset with the gold.
recordings.drill: Next game, after inhibitor/base pressure, say 'free, blocked, or reset' before every forward click for the next 20 seconds.
timeline: 20:58 - Samira is in the enemy base with allied pressure and an open lane; this is the objective habit to keep.

27:56 - Samira re-enters a base fight at low HP into multiple defenders, the exact shutdown/stall pattern to cut.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: the leak was not ignoring objectives; it was staying available after the first won sequence when the next clean payout was reset or structure only. Fix: free structure means hit it, blocked structure means kill only the blocker, and shutdown gold or one-hit HP means reset before another fight.
recordings.gameDetail: That is the leak: the first win already gave value, but staying available makes the next fight worth more for them than for you. At 11:14 Samira and the wave are near the enemy Nexus turret, which is the correct objective shape to keep. The lesson is not never fight after inhibitor; it is to separate free structure, blocked body, and reset before clicking forward again.

recordings.goodThing: You did route pressure back into structures; the 11:14 Nexus-turret setup is the objective habit to keep.
recordings.whyTrust: This separates the objective discipline you did show from the part that still leaks tempo, so the fix does not punish the habit that was already improving.
recordings.evidence: 11:14 Samira near enemy Nexus turrets with vision circles—shows post-inhib structure taken, holding shutdown gold, and the risky overstay window instead of recalling to spend, directly supporting the overstay/recall discipline lesson.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Overstay After Fight Win Near Inhibitor
recordings.feedback: Mistake: Stayed mid and pushed into grouped enemies after breaking the inhibitor, instead of recalling to spend massive gold and play for next spawn/timer. Fix: After breaking inhibitor or mid tower, immediately recall if you are sitting on high gold and enemy spawns are coming up, instead of fighting or clearing one more wave under risk.
recordings.gameDetail: This let gold sit unspent for almost two minutes and risked feed/lethal resets. Resetting immediately after inhibitor opens lets your next push/Baron play break the base with items and safety. Lesson: Always convert a big objective win into a reset before enemy respawn, unless the Nexus is free.
recordings.eventEvidence: 14:01 Samira is still mid with allies and enemies near inhibitor after it's broken, instead of recalling with massive gold—shows the overstay after objective, not resetting for next play.
recordings.goodThing: Executed on the inhibitor tower opportunity and converted a fight win into a structure.
recordings.whyTrust: Almost every Samira throw in this ELO comes from overstay after a won fight; resetting with a 3k+ gold spend and regrouping is the single most repeatable win conversion and prevents stall throws.
recordings.evidence: 14:01 Samira is still mid with allies and enemies near inhibitor after it's broken, instead of recalling with massive gold—shows the overstay after objective, not resetting for next play.
recordings.pattern: Successfully breaks objective after a fight and keeps pushing for another wave or more, even with huge accumulated gold and enemy respawns. Delay between taking open structure and actually resetting leaves team exposed if enemy groups.
recordings.diamondRule: After breaking open inhibitor or tower and sitting at over 2000g, recall and spend before next objective, unless the Nexus is open.
recordings.drill: Next game, after breaking any inhibitor or inner tower and having over 2000g, immediately recall, spend, and only return for objective or grouped fight.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: after surviving pressure, you kept treating the map as playable instead of choosing the guaranteed payout. Fix: if the inhibitor is free, hit it; if you are too chunked to keep playing, recall before contesting another wave.

The better check is structure first, then recall if the next structure is not immediately free.

The better check is watching the enemy respawn timers and disengaging immediately after the inhib falls. This keeps your tempo and stops the enemy from farming comeback gold.

recordings.evidence: 19:05 Samira scores a triple kill deep near the enemy nexus after the inhibitor is already taken, with most enemies respawning—shows clear overstay and risk rather than resetting with a lead.

At 14:33 the chain reaches triple, at 14:38 the quadra happens beside the nexus area, and at 14:41 the penta lands because the fight never leaves the ending window. recordings.feedbackTitle: You stay when one hit kills you
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing the wave when one auto or spell kills you. Fix: give the wave and recall before the lane turns into a death timer.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: this is not a mechanics issue; it is a health-gate issue. At 2:43 Samira is under bot tower with the enemy wave arriving, Ashe still visible to the right, and enough missing HP that the lane should already be treated as dangerous instead of playable. At 3:27 the real mistake is clearer: Samira is still beside the bot wave at lethal health, the camera and movement are staying with minions, and the recall path has not been taken even though one clean enemy touch can turn the wave into a death timer. At 4:57 the good version is visible after the reset: Samira is back on the map with enough HP to walk forward, Q/auto from range, and choose the next trade instead of begging not to be hit. By 14:28 the team is ending with Samira alive around the base fight, which proves the champion damage is not the blocker. The blocker is staying in lane after the health bar says the next decision should be recall.
recordings.eventEvidence: 2:43 Samira is under bot tower with Ashe still visible and the wave arriving; 3:27 Samira is still in lane at lethal health; 4:57 reset gives enough HP to re-enter; 14:28 the later team ending works.
recordings.goodThing: After the bad early health decision, you still got back into the game and converted later pressure into the ending push.
recordings.whyTrust: This is not vague advice: the reviewed frames show the exact one-hit-health lane stay, and removing that habit protects the aggression that already works later.

recordings.title: Missed Immediate Structure Convert After Kill
recordings.feedbackTitle: Missed Immediate Structure Convert After Kill
recordings.feedback: Mistake: After solo killing Kayle mid, you did not immediately hit the open mid inhibitor turret to convert your lead. Fix: Instantly turn on and damage the exposed turret or inhibitor after clearing enemy threats when the wave is at tower.
recordings.gameDetail: As the wave crashes under mid inhibitor and Kayle is killed solo by Samira, the enemy mid structure is completely exposed, but Samira moves to clear minions instead of attacking the open tower. There are no enemy defenders present mid, minions are at the building, but the free tempo window is burned by autopiloting for farm. The better next click is to hit the inhibitor turret right after securing the pick, as it opens up the base and applies map pressure. When you win a fight or catch, hit the structure instantly instead of defaulting to more minion hits.
recordings.eventEvidence: 13:48 Samira kills Kayle with the mid inhibitor turret exposed and does not immediately hit the free structure, illustrating the missed convert after a successful pick.
recordings.goodThing: You successfully converted a solo kill under pressure, showing mechanical confidence and winning a 1v1.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira's kit rewards snowballing through towers and inhibitors, and the proof here is a completely open structure with no visible enemy contest after a successful kill.
recordings.evidence: 13:48 Samira kills Kayle with the mid inhibitor turret exposed and does not immediately hit the free structure, illustrating the missed convert after a successful pick.
recordings.pattern: After a successful solo kill with the wave at the enemy base, you defaulted to more farm rather than urgent tower pressure. This leaves map pressure unclaimed.
recordings.diamondRule: Always convert Samira advantage into structure pressure when the enemy inhibitor or turret is open and no defenders are present.
recordings.drill: After every mid lane kill with a wave at the inhibitor, immediately right-click the turret, not minions.

recordings.title: Respect missing and incoming TPs
recordings.feedbackTitle: Respect missing and incoming TPs
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed too far forward in lane and did not respect the enemy Teleport coming in while exposed. Fix: When your ADC is hitting the wave and the enemy TP is coming down, immediately back off from melee range and give ground until you confirm numbers and threat.
recordings.gameDetail: The wave was pushing forward with Caitlyn present and Samira (Alan) in melee range of the enemy caster minions, with both Caitlyn and Samira pushing just past river. A Teleport appears directly into the lane, yet Samira does not back off—instead, Alan is caught in the middle of the wave and is immediately crowd-controlled and killed, giving a shutdown. The better response is to immediately pull back as soon as TP is channeled, especially when ahead and holding a shutdown, completely avoiding the play. Around 3:37, enemy Teleport channels onto a minion while Samira stands in melee range, gets stunned—shows the mistake of not backing off as TP appears; By 3:40, Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of not respecting the incoming Teleport and staying too far forward. Always respect missing enemies and incoming Teleports when you are far up in lane.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:37 Enemy Teleport channels onto a minion while Samira stands in melee range, gets stunned—shows the mistake of not backing off as TP appears.; 3:40 Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of not respecting the incoming Teleport and staying too far forward.
recordings.goodThing: You and your lane partner pushed the wave aggressively to pressure the enemy tower.
recordings.whyTrust: Getting caught by TP like this instantly swings tempo and gold back to the enemy—this is a punishable, avoidable error and safe habits here directly stop throwing leads.
recordings.evidence: 3:37 Enemy Teleport channels onto a minion while Samira stands in melee range, gets stunned—shows the mistake of not backing off as TP appears.; 3:40 Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of not respecting the incoming Teleport and staying too far forward.
recordings.pattern: Positioning aggressively in lane with a shutdown lead but not reacting to cross-map threats or Teleports results in immediate punish; the kill was 100% avoidable with minimal discipline.
recordings.diamondRule: If you see a Teleport coming in near you and you don't have numbers, back off instantly—never greed wave or aggression with shutdown gold exposed.
recordings.drill: In your next game, back away instantly on every enemy Teleport in your lane, even if you think they might not have kill pressure—build the habit of never greeting TP plays.

recordings.title: Reset after the turret falls
recordings.feedbackTitle: Reset after the turret falls
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you are dead as the red turret falls, so the objective win turns into a stagger instead of a clean reset. Fix: once the turret drops, exit with the wave or recall with the team before the next enemy bodies arrive.
recordings.gameDetail: The objective went through, so the good part is that the team got structure value; the leak is dying during or right after that conversion, which removes your ability to reset, spend, and help the next map play. The better decision after the turret falls is exit or recall, not staying in the lane long enough to become the stagger. The lesson is to make the objective win end the sequence cleanly.
recordings.goodThing: Your team broke the inhibitor turret, creating an opportunity for a powerful map reset or further pressure.
recordings.whyTrust: This death costs Samira both tempo and agency on the map, but the correct reset after a turret break directly leads to more vision and better drag/baron prep in every rank.
recordings.pattern: The structure conversion happened, but the death means the sequence did not end cleanly; the next improvement is exiting after the payout.
recordings.diamondRule: When the turret falls, the sequence is over unless the next structure is free right now.
recordings.drill: After every turret take, click back once and check recall before looking for another fight.
nuance: Good: the team still took the turret.
Leak: Samira was dead during the payout instead of resetting after it.
Consequence: the structure win becomes a stagger, so the next objective setup is weaker.
Next check: turret falls, exit or recall unless the next structure is free immediately.
recordings.reviewLimit: This short clip has one reviewable frame, so it supports the turret-death timing but not the full fight setup.
